feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Process Integration
feed.subtitle: SAP Process Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to regenerate the system certificates in sap po 7.5?
entry.content:
Hi Team,
Good day!

We have a SAP standard system certificate expiring on March 17th, in SAP PO Quality and Production.

Please can you provide new certificate to renew it timely for avoiding any disconnections. Kindly provide us the procedure to update the certificates. in SAP PO landscape.

Thanks & Regards
Gopi


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Dynamically extract a field value & then insert it to a field on SOAP Header of async respons
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have requirement that has to be acheived on SAP PO/PI .. this is regarding asynchronous request ans asynch response scenario.. where in the asnych request message we receive "MessageID" field value in SOAP envelope header and there is an asynchronous response where that MessageID value from request message has to be extracted & then insert it to another field "RelatesTo" of asynch response (a field on SOAP Header level).. Could you please guide me to acheive this requirement in SAP PO 7.5 single stack?

Sample asynch request

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<soapenv:Envelope xmlns:soapenv="" xmlns:soapenc="" xmlns:xsd="" xmlns:xsi="">
<soapenv:Header xmlns:wsa= xmlns:wsse="">
<wsa:MessageID>f3d02d20e2821edfbaa386b6c489d7de</wsa:MessageID>
<wsa:RelatesTo>f3d02d20e2821edfbaa386b6c489d7de</wsa:RelatesTo>
</soapenv:Header>
<soapenv:Body>
<ns0:cancelRequest>
<ns1:PIwort>
</ns1:PIwort>
</ns1:cancelRequest>

Sample asynch response

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<soapenv:Envelope xmlns:soapenv="" xmlns:soapenc="" xmlns:xsd="" xmlns:xsi="">
<soapenv:Header xmlns:wsa="" xmlns:wsse="">
<wsse:Security soapenv:actor="engbus" soapenv:mustUnderstand="1">
</wsse:UsernameToken>
</wsse:Security>
<wsa:From>
</wsa:From>
<wsa:Action>urn:Preisindikation/confirmOrder</wsa:Action>
<wsa:RelatesTo>f3d02d20e2821edfbaa386b6c489d7de</wsa:RelatesTo>
</soapenv:Header>
<soapenv:Body>
<ns0:cancelResponse>
<ns2:Buenummer>08-001</ns2:Buenummer>
</ns1:cancelResponse>

Could you please let me guide me to acheive this requirement?

 

Thanks

Ramu

entry.title: PI issue
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a question regarding SQL DB to S4HANA Integration using JDBC to Proxy scenario in SAP PO/PI .. Here, the Databricks DB guys have shared a very lengthy SQL statement but it is not compatible with SAP PO. should we ask DB guys to build stored procedure on their side?

Is it possible to call stored procedure using JDBC sender adapter from PO?

 

Thanks

Ramu

entry.title: Documents not integrated between TPV and SAP
entry.content:
hello in the company we are relatively new using SAPB1 and especially TPV, we had an incident with one of the teams and due to ignorance, we took a step that harmed us with the integration of TPV documents to SAP and we don't see how we can correct the incident.

 

Documents are not integrated because they indicate that related documents already exist.

 

I show you in the picture:

 

Has this happened to anyone in the forum? You may be able to guide me with a solution to this type of incident? 

 

Thank you in advance for the help

 

Best regards

Harvey Oropeza.

entry.title: SAP PO (Process Orchestration) to SAP CPI (Cloud Platform Integration) Migration
entry.content:
Motivation


SAP Process Orchestration (PO) supports custom process applications and integration scenarios for a lot of SAP customers. It evolved over many years to a very stable and feature rich integration solution. Because of changing business requirements and your IT strategy, it could be a good idea to check, if SAP Integration Suite supports your processes and integration requirements better?

If you consider moving to SAP Integration Suite, you might ask yourself what is the best time to start a project for your migration?

Benefits of SAP Integration Suite


SAP outlines some benefits why customers should migrate and transform with SAP BTP  / SAP Integration Suite:

  • SAP Integration Suite provides the flexibility and independence you need by delivering technology to move assets to the cloud, integrate the IT landscape, and configure and extend SAP and third-party applications.

     

    • Achieve seamless business processes: Integrate processes and data across diverse application landscapes and business networks with comprehensive integration capabilities.
      Accelerate business outcomes: Quickly integrate and connect any application or business partner with thousands of pre-built integrations using a proven methodology based on 50 years of SAP best practices.

       

    • Modernize integration: Enable self-service integration at scale with simpler tools, built-in AI, and modern integration capabilities. Deploy to any cloud with trust and security fully managed by SAP.

       

      SAP Assessment and Migration Tool


      It was possible to move to SAP Integration Suite in a - manual way - by migration SAP PI /PO IFlows and ICos to Integration Flows in CPI (former name of Cloud Integration) since years. By January 23 SAP released their SAP Assessment and Migration Tool to support migration projects. So is a migration from SAP PO to SAP Integration Suite now a "click of a button"? I think that is with the current version of the solution not possible, because some functionality is missing and a migration of the complete content will not be possible and sometime does not make sense. But you get a good overview on your integration content in SAP Process Orchestration with a rough estimation of your effort to move to SAP Integration Suite.

      The first step will be to connect you SA PO system via Cloud Connector in the Migration Assessment tool like described in SAP Help.

      After that you can start with extracting data from your system

       

       

       

       

      Extract Data in SAP Assessment Tool

       

      When the extraction job is finished, you can create a based on the data via:

       

       

       

       

       


      Scenario Evaluation


      This will show you a nice dashboard, but also the opportunity to download the results via Excel or PDF.

       

       

       

       

       

       

      Dashboard

       

       

       

       

       

       

       

      Download Results

       

      The next steps will be to evaluate the results and estimation and perhaps identify some scenarios, where you will have some quick wins or easy to migrate ones. If there are special requirements from your side, you can check the rules in the settings section.


      If you identified some flows, you would like to migrate, you are able to directly start with the Migration Tool. That is not designed as central tool with a central dashboard to push content to Cloud Integration but more as pulling flows from SAP PO to specific packages in Cloud Integration. So first of all, create a new package and the click on "Migrate":

       

       

       

       

       

       

      Migrate in Cloud Integration


      After selecting a flow and some more clicks, you migrated your first flow from SAP PO to Cloud Integration 

       

       

       

       

       

       

       

       

      first migrated flow


      For sure, you can do the migration manually, but at the end a tool does it in a reproducible und save way. There are some restrictions today, so that you need to do some adjustments manually. To make it more and more sures, I would suggest doing at least some testing and send feedback to SAP in case of issues or missing features. Some generic ideas and information’s can we found here.

      Please keep in mind, that there is no status update for migrated flows today. So consider to mark them in PO in an appropriate way.

      SAP Integration Suite vs. Cloud Integration


      Integration became more complex in comparison that was there, when SAP PO was designed. So I think a migration from SAP PO to Cloud Integration is not the whole story: There are much more holistic capabilities in SAP Integration Suite and it makes sense to perhaps rethink the implemented scenarios and to migrate from SAP PO to SAP Integration Suite, e.g. to:

      • add a central API proxy as entry point for more security or a better developer experience

         

      • use Event Mesh for high volume interface or to implement loose coupling
      • implement a CAP service for some central logic, that could be reused.

        Unit, Integration and Regression Testing
        In migration projects the implementation takes time, but typically testing is a a big block where you need integration and business experts. Because business experts have a lot do what delivers more values than doing tests manually, it could be very a booster for your project to automate testing as much as possible:

      • Unit test manually via simulation and test tool
      • Integration testing via INT4 Shield with PO and Cloud Integration in one step
      • Use integration tests for regressions tests also after migration project

        If you don't have solutions in place for that, consider to use the 12 month free license of the Figaf tool or the INT4 Shield.

        Migration Approaches
        When thinking a bit on migration options or strategies, I find a analogy to S/4 projects very interesting:

      • Brownfield conversion: More or less try to copy/migrate as much as possible content from SAP PO to Cloud Integration without changing the logic of implemented integrations. This could be a good option in case of many standard integrations or if are not able to change thing e.g. because of keeping migration costs low.
      • Selective: Copy the mass of interface in an intelligent way and implement changes, where it brings quick wins e.g. by using more flexible functionality compared to SAP PO. One idea could be to implement a API security and load balancing layer or to document your interfaces via API Hub Business Portal (OpenAPI)
      • Greenfield: Start with ISA-M process to design your Integration landscape of the future. After that, build some interfaces based on the new guidelines, learn about the new capabilities and how to handle them in live production. With these experiences build packages of interfaces and migrate them in waves to Integration. Perhaps you can use the best of PO and Integration for a while to have a smooth migration?

        Migration project steps / phases
        Perhaps we can reuse SAP Activate for our migration projects?

      • Discover: The outcome of the Discover phase is to lean about the new SAP Integration Suite capabilities an do define your Integration approach. Also, a basic documentation of your landscape should be ready afterwards.
      • Prepare: The outcome of the SAP Prepare phase is to setup the Assessment tool (see above) and to run the assessment on your SAP PO system(s). You should analyse the results in detail regarding effort and what is possible to migration automatically. During the Prepare phase, SAP or enterprise architects will build a business case for migration, which outlines the benefits and reasons for migrating from SAP PO to Integration Suite.
      • Explore: A change impact analysis will be completed to understand how SAP Integration Suite will benefit the business and integration team. This outcome of the Explore phase is to enable a smooth transition to the new system.
      • Realize: SAP functional consultants and business users will work together to ensure the objects in Integration Suite are delivered within the timeline and to a high standard. Once all the deliverables are satisfied, a cutover plan is made to move the configurations.
      • Deploy: The outcome of the Deploy phase is to migrate the interfaces to SAP Integration Suite so that customers and partners can benefit from the new capabilities while continuing to receive support in case of problems or concerns.
      • Run: The main task for consultants during the Run phase is to keep up to date with the latest innovation and technologies in SAP as part of the product’s “Continued Learning” philosophy. Once the transformed integration landscape is up and running, the focus shifts to continuous improvement and risk management.

        SAP PO to SAP Integration Suite Migration 
        So finally, what to do? Start now? Migrate everything in one step? Or do it step by step? What is the best way to do it and what is the best target architecture?

        I think it makes sense nearly for every customer to START NOW, because:

      • we now have the tools form SAP in GA
      • there are quick wins by SAP Integration Suite nearly for every scenario
      • it is a complex project, that takes time and will be become more difficult, when starting at the end of SAP PO lifetime

        For sure, there are still missing capabilities in the Migration- and Assessment tool and also in SAP Integration Suite (compared to SAP PO). That's we I would suggest starting now, to migrate what makes sense in you landscape and to use the power of SAP PO and SAP Integration perhaps for a certain time until needed features are available.

         

         

      •  

      •  

      •  

      •  

      •  

    •

entry.title: IDOC metadata issue on SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have IDOC to proxy interface on SAP PO 7.5 single stack. IDOC structure on sender SAP ECC system got changed i.e, under segment under E1MARAM segment SAP ECC started sending MATNR_LONG instead of MATNR. What should we do from SAP PO side.. should we ask SAP ECC colleauges to share IDOC schema? then import that IDOC schema into SAP PO/PI to make the structure consistent on SAP PO/PI & SAP backend? which format SAP ECC guys should share the IDOC schema XSD or WSDL? what are the options available on their end?

 

Thanks

Ramu

entry.title: Null pointer Exception when using Error handling in rest receiver channel im SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi, 

My scenario is proxy -> synchronous REST.

The REST service I'm calling returns an empty message when the input data is incorrect, including error information in custom headers.

This generates an exception in PO: java.io.IOException: java.io.EOFException

In the error handling tab in the rest receiver channel, when using "Text Content" -> "Result Message is Empty", I still get an EOFException.

The only way to work around this is to use the "Exception" option in "error handling". It should work fine, but when using the "Exception" option in the "Error Handling" tab, we get the following exception in message monitoring:

java.lang.NullPointerException: When trying to invoke the java.lang.CharSequence.toString() method on a null object loaded from the local variable 'replacement'

Any ideas on how to resolve this null pointer exception?

It happens no matter what kind of message I put in "Message Content".

Thanks And regards.

Alex-

entry.title: Messages in HOLDING status SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have messages in HOLDING status since long time ago (7 months ago)..

Could you please guide on how to resolve the errors on SAP PO?

Because we cant either resend/delete/cancel these messages from SAP PO app..

Thanks

Ramu

entry.title: Does switching from exactly once to at least once semantics have a peformance impact in PI
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to improve the performance in terms of messages processed a secound in PI by scaling up.

i.e increase the number of nodes, threads, parallism of the REST and Kafka adapter involved.

I was wondering if the Exactly Once Semantics in PI generates more load compared to At Least Once Semantics.

Does anyone have any view/experience on performance and use of system resources of these PI Semantics.

regards

Angelo

entry.title: How to divide XML message records (node) wise and post it one by one to a REST API using SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a scenario in SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) where I'm fetching project data from SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition) using an OData call. The response XML contains multiple <entry> records, each representing a project.

<feed xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom" xmlns:m="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata" xmlns:d="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices" xml:base="https://my******-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/YY1_PROJECTDETAILS_API_CDS/">
    <id>https://-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/YY1_PROJECTDETAILS_API_CDS/YY1_ProjectDetails_API</id>
    <title type="text">YY1_ProjectDetails_API</title>
    <updated>2025-04-07T05:59:03Z</updated>
    <author>
        <name/>
    </author>
    <link href="YY1_ProjectDetails_API" rel="self" title="YY1_ProjectDetails_API"/>
    <entry>
        <id>https://my*****-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/YY1_PROJECTDETAILS_API_CDS/YY1_ProjectDetails_API(guid'b702a842-56ed-1eef-b7a8-9f7fe6c49bb6')</id>
        <title type="text">YY1_ProjectDetails_API(guid'b702a842-56ed-1eef-b7a8-9f7fe6c49bb6')</title>
        <updated>2025-04-07T05:59:03Z</updated>
        <category term="YY1_PROJECTDETAILS_API_CDS.YY1_ProjectDetails_APIType" scheme="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/scheme"/>
        <link href="YY1_ProjectDetails_API(guid'b702a842-56ed-1eef-b7a8-9f7fe6c49bb6')" rel="self" title="YY1_ProjectDetails_APIType"/>
        <link href="YY1_ProjectDetails_API(guid'b702a842-56ed-1eef-b7a8-9f7fe6c49bb6')/to_YY1_IsSynced_PPH" rel="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/related/to_YY1_IsSynced_PPH" type="application/atom+xml;type=entry" title="to_YY1_IsSynced_PPH"/>
        <content type="application/xml">
            <m:properties xmlns:m="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata" xmlns:d="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices">
                <d:ProjectUUID>b702a842-56ed-1eef-b7a8-9f7fe6c49bb6</d:ProjectUUID>
                <d:ProjectDescription>Adaptive 26/11 Prd Dev</d:ProjectDescription>
                <d:Project>E-91-ARA-PRD-2611</d:Project>
                <d:ProjectStartDate>2024-12-01T00:00:00</d:ProjectStartDate>
                <d:ProjectEndDate>2052-04-16T00:00:00</d:ProjectEndDate>
                <d:ProfitCenter>9150A30000</d:ProfitCenter>
                <d:CompanyCode>2000</d:CompanyCode>
                <d:ResponsibleCostCenter>9150A30000</d:ResponsibleCostCenter>
                <d:ProjectStatus>Created</d:ProjectStatus>
                <d:Person>9980000001</d:Person>
                <d:PersonFullName>User A</d:PersonFullName>
                <d:ProjectRoleName>Project Financial Controller</d:ProjectRoleName>
                <d:YY1_Customer2_PPH></d:YY1_Customer2_PPH>
                <d:YY1_IsSynced_PPH></d:YY1_IsSynced_PPH>
            </m:properties>
        </content>
    </entry>
    <entry>
        <id>https://my*****-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/YY1_PROJECTDETAILS_API_CDS/YY1_ProjectDetails_API(guid'33382281-a32f-1eef-b4b5-3d515802dbb0')</id>
        <title type="text">YY1_ProjectDetails_API(guid'33382281-a32f-1eef-b4b5-3d515802dbb0')</title>
        <updated>2025-04-07T05:59:03Z</updated>
        <category term="YY1_PROJECTDETAILS_API_CDS.YY1_ProjectDetails_APIType" scheme="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/scheme"/>
        <link href="YY1_ProjectDetails_API(guid'33382281-a32f-1eef-b4b5-3d515802dbb0')" rel="self" title="YY1_ProjectDetails_APIType"/>
        <link href="YY1_ProjectDetails_API(guid'33382281-a32f-1eef-b4b5-3d515802dbb0')/to_YY1_IsSynced_PPH" rel="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/related/to_YY1_IsSynced_PPH" type="application/atom+xml;type=entry" title="to_YY1_IsSynced_PPH"/>
        <content type="application/xml">
            <m:properties xmlns:m="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata" xmlns:d="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices">
                <d:ProjectUUID>33382281-a32f-1eef-b4b5-3d515802dbb0</d:ProjectUUID>
                <d:ProjectDescription>Customized Engineering with BMW</d:ProjectDescription>
                <d:Project>E-49-ENG-BMW-25</d:Project>
                <d:ProjectStartDate>2023-04-01T00:00:00</d:ProjectStartDate>
                <d:ProjectEndDate>2026-12-31T00:00:00</d:ProjectEndDate>
                <d:ProfitCenter>4960A00001</d:ProfitCenter>
                <d:CompanyCode>1000</d:CompanyCode>
                <d:ResponsibleCostCenter>4960A00001</d:ResponsibleCostCenter>
                <d:ProjectStatus>Released</d:ProjectStatus>
                <d:Person>9980000011</d:Person>
                <d:PersonFullName>Aakanksha</d:PersonFullName>
                <d:ProjectRoleName>Project Financial Controller</d:ProjectRoleName>
                <d:YY1_Customer2_PPH>1000023</d:YY1_Customer2_PPH>
                <d:YY1_IsSynced_PPH></d:YY1_IsSynced_PPH>
            </m:properties>
        </content>
    </entry>
    <entry>
        <id>https://my*****-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/YY1_PROJECTDETAILS_API_CDS/YY1_ProjectDetails_API(guid'b702a842-56ed-1eef-b7a8-9f7fe6c49bb6')</id>

    <entry>
        <id>https://my*****-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/YY1_PROJECTDETAILS_API_CDS/YY1_ProjectDetails_API(guid'b702a842-56ed-1eef-bad3-7eb2630a7bb6')</id>
        <title type="text">YY1_ProjectDetails_API(guid'b702a842-56ed-1eef-bad3-7eb2630a7bb6')</title>
        <updated>2025-04-07T05:59:03Z</updated>
        <category term="YY1_PROJECTDETAILS_API_CDS.YY1_ProjectDetails_APIType" scheme="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/scheme"/>
        <link href="YY1_ProjectDetails_API(guid'b702a842-56ed-1eef-bad3-7eb2630a7bb6')" rel="self" title="YY1_ProjectDetails_APIType"/>
        <link href="YY1_ProjectDetails_API(guid'b702a842-56ed-1eef-bad3-7eb2630a7bb6')/to_YY1_IsSynced_PPH" rel="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/related/to_YY1_IsSynced_PPH" type="application/atom+xml;type=entry" title="to_YY1_IsSynced_PPH"/>
        <content type="application/xml">
            <m:properties xmlns:m="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata" xmlns:d="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices">
                <d:ProjectUUID>b702a842-56ed-1eef-bad3-7eb2630a7bb6</d:ProjectUUID>
                <d:ProjectDescription>Test Project</d:ProjectDescription>
                <d:Project>TESTPROJECT1</d:Project>
                <d:ProjectStartDate>2025-02-01T00:00:00</d:ProjectStartDate>
                <d:ProjectEndDate>2025-02-04T00:00:00</d:ProjectEndDate>
                <d:ProfitCenter>9150A10000</d:ProfitCenter>
                <d:CompanyCode>2000</d:CompanyCode>
                <d:ResponsibleCostCenter>9150A10000</d:ResponsibleCostCenter>
                <d:ProjectStatus>Released</d:ProjectStatus>
                <d:Person>9980000001</d:Person>
                <d:PersonFullName>User</d:PersonFullName>
                <d:ProjectRoleName>Project Financial Controller</d:ProjectRoleName>
                <d:YY1_Customer2_PPH></d:YY1_Customer2_PPH>
                <d:YY1_IsSynced_PPH></d:YY1_IsSynced_PPH>
            </m:properties>
        </content>
    </entry>
</feed>

The requirement is to send each project record individually to a third-party REST API (3rd Party HRMS), which only accepts one record per request in application/x-www-form-urlencoded format with the key name inputData.

I tried using Iterating Splitter, but I am not getting the expected behavior — it's not making separate HTTP calls or invoking the Request Reply as intended.

My goal is:

  • Fetch XML containing multiple projects

  • Split it into individual messages (each project)

  • Send one-by-one using a Request Reply step to the API

  • Handle and log each response if needed

    Any step-by-step help, example flow, or blog reference would be highly appreciated.

    Thanks in advance! 
    Best regards,
    Prathmesh Sutar

entry.title: Problem after migration TREX
entry.content:
Dear Support,

During the migration of TREX, we recreated the indexes with the same ID as the original system, but it takes a long time to transition from the "to be preprocessed" state to the "to be indexed" state.

The indexing occurs, but very slowly.

What can we do?

entry.title: Error : Could Not Determine mapping steps for message in SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

I have a IDOC -> PI -> SFTP   scenario that I have implemented with an ICO (I'm using PI 7.50).

while testing i am getting the following error.

Transmitting the message to endpoint <local> using connection AFW failed, due to: com.sap.engine.interfaces.messaging.api.exception.MessagingException: Error encountered while executing mapping: com.sap.aii.af.service.mapping.MappingException: com.sap.aii.utilxi.misc.api.ResourceException: Could not determine mapping steps for message 7e24f305-1f9e-11f0-c2e8-00000c37e086

But it works fine in production not in consolidate system.

please check the message logs of particular message

Please help .

Regards,

Ravi

entry.title: Hi, can I directly access dynamicconfiguration variable inside Integrated Configuration?
entry.content:
Here is my PI solution. 
1. it's a pass through PI solution (REST Adapter) for GET and POST calls. No ESR. 
2. We deliver a TPZ file to customers. No separate adapter module deployment. 
3. In our GET calls, we pass a bunch of HTTP Headers. 

One challenge we had with our customers is that some customers require HTTP Header Content-Type and some don't in GET/DELETE calls. In CI, there's automatic forwarding of HTTP headers from source to target. 
In PI, I understand that I have to list out all the HTTP Headers that I want to pass to downstream system. This means that Content-Type will always be on the header to downstream system regardless if it's provided by sender or not. 
The workaround we came up with is 2 receiver communication channels. 1 with Content-Type and 1 without Content-Type. We currently also have 2 sender communication channels. We would like to set a condition inside Integrated Configuration to determine the appropriate receiver communication channel based on the presence of Content-Type. I made it work with ContextObject HEADER.ID2 by passing the HTTP Header Content-Type to it.  

What we would like is NOT to use HEADER.ID2, but our own variable from DynamicConfiguration. 
In my below screenshot, we have tau_header_content_type and header_content_type. Can I use one of these directly in XPath?

I tried
/DynamicConfiguration/sap:Record[@name='tau_header_content_type']
/DynamicConfiguration/tau_header_content_type
and a bunch of other variations, but they all give me an error.

entry.title: SAP PI/PO Dynamic Configuration error while creating an attribute with more than 4096 characters
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We have a PROXY to REST synchronous interface in SAP PI 7.5 landscape (SP Stack Number 28).

The working of the interface is as follows:

1. SAP sends message to PI via Proxy.
2. PI gets the token via lookup call to an authentication server from Java Mapping.
3. Token is passed to a header field via Dynamic Configuration from Java Mapping.
4. PI then calls the API with the token received through REST Receiver adapter.

This interface has been working smoothly for the last 6 years. But recently we have observed that the token returned by the authentication server has the length around 4100 characters and the interface has stopped working. It is throwing exception in the java mapping while modifying the attribute through dynamic configuration.

Dynamic Configuration part of the Java Mapping:

DynamicConfiguration conf = inputObj.getDynamicConfiguration();

DynamicConfigurationKey key = DynamicConfigurationKey.create("http://sap.com/xi/XI/System/REST","XHeaderName1");

conf.put(key, token);

 

 

Error:

Attempted Solution (not worked):

We already tried to split the token into two parts and write it in two headers (token1 = XHeaderName1 and token2 = XHeaderName2). However, while putting {token1}{token2} in accesstoken header, an error is saying it would expect a value in between {token1} and {token2}.

Please let me know if anyone faced the similar issue earlier and how to resolve this.

entry.title: SSL Handshake error
entry.content:
SSL Handshake error persists even after appropriate certificates have been imported to Trusted CA.

Error description:

"MP: exception caught with cause com.sap.aii.adapter.rest.ejb.security.SSLException: SSL-enabled call failed. Error while configuring SSL client Error retrieving certificates:"

entry.title: SAP PI - Group records based on values of the source message
entry.content:
Hi all,

Ive an requirement to group and filter based on the source values:

Ive the following nested Stucture that needs to be mapped/grouped and filtered to the target nested structure:

The Source message can be like this:

And the target XML should look like this:

  • When there is only TestId, the TestId and its Parameter should be passed.
  • When there is both SampleId and TestId, only SampleId should be passed and all the corresponding Parameters of that SampleId should be grouped on. In this case 3 parameters for SampleId (S1) and 1 Parameter for SampleId (S2).
  • Also when there is no TargetId field, the corresponding parameters shouldnt be passed (like parameter P3).

My preference is graphical mapping...
I would be appreciated if you can help me with this, 

Thank you,
John

entry.title: MFA setup for SAP PO/PI risks
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are planning to implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for logging into our SAP PO/PI systems. We have around 230+ partner systems connected to our SAP backend via SAP PO/PI. Could you please let me know if there are any risks associated with this, particularly regarding potential impacts on connectivity with these partner systems?

Regards, 

Ramu.

entry.title: Troubles with IDOC and write modus append for SFTP server
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I have next problem. Delivering files (IDOCs) through PO from SAP system and trying to append them into 1 file. Problem comes when client is connecting server too early and pick file when it is not yet fully completed.

Is there any solutions how to make it more safe? Maybe some one had same situation?

Br,

Andrejs

entry.title: Unable to Activate Agreement in B2B Scenarios
entry.content:
Hello Folks,

I created a MIG with a custom X12 structure. When I tried to use this in trading partner agreement and activate it , I am getting the below error.

Error was : Failed to publish Message Implementation Guideline with id e4147ff852204af8902494b688e8b75c to Partner Directory. Error was : Error executing [PUSH_TO_PD] on service [SAP Integration Advisor]. Response was [Code: 500, Reason : Internal Server Error : {"errorCode":"FAILED_TO_PUSH_ARTIFACT_TO_CPI","errorMessage":"Destination it-cpi034_CPI_DEST could not be loaded."}]

Can anyone please help ?

entry.title: rate limiting in sap po
entry.content:
hello

is rate limiting possible in sap po 7.5?

we have a requirement where we need to restrict number of calls made towards sap webservice .

example if consumer is making more than 1000 calls/hour towards sap .we need to send them back too many request error in response.but this is for only one particular interface not in general for all sap po interface

is it possible to implement such feature in sap po ?

Thanks,
Sahana
